target_case.question: Who was the professor from the private Ph.D.-granting research university in Chicago that founded a website that is a multimedia archive of the United States Supreme court?
target_case.summary_1: Jerry Goldman — research professor of law at Chicago‑Kent College of Law (Illinois Institute of Technology); founded the Oyez Project, an online multimedia archive of the U.S. Supreme Court (Oyez Project).
target_case.hop2_query: Jerry Goldman Oyez Project founder Chicago-Kent College of Law

retrieved_doc_snippets: Oyez Project | The Oyez Project at the Illinois Institute of Technology's Chicago-Kent College of Law is an unofficial online multimedia archive of the Supreme Court of the United States, especially audio of oral arguments. The website also includes biographical information of both incumbent and historical justices of the Court and advocates who have argued  ...[truncated]

Chicago-Kent Journal of Intellectual Property | The Chicago-Kent Journal of Intellectual Property ("Bluebook" abbreviation: "Chi.-Kent J. Intell. Prop") is a student-run law journal published at Chicago-Kent College of Law since 1999. It covers all aspects of intellectual property law, including patent law, trademark law, copyright law, and trade secrets. Journal issues are available on Westlaw, LexisNexis, and HeinOnline. The journal also has a blog presenting short summaries and updates on int ...[truncated]

Chicago-Kent College of Law | Chicago-Kent College of Law is a law school affiliated with the Illinois Institute of Technology. It is the second oldest law school in the state of Illinois. Its trial advocacy program is ranked in 2015 by "U.S. News and World Report" as the fourth best program in the country. According to Chicago-Kent's 2014 American Bar Association-required disclosures, 85% of the 2014 class secured a position six months after graduation. Of these 248 employed graduates, 172 were ...[truncated]

Center on Nanotechnology and Society | The Center on Nanotechnology and Society (Nano & Society) is an affiliate of Illinois Institute of Technology (IIT) and is housed at IIT’s Chicago-Kent College of Law. Nano & Society is an affiliate of the Institute on Biotechnology and the Human Future, also based at Chicago-Kent College of Law.

Richard Kling | Richard Kling is a Clinical Professor of Law at the Chicago–Kent College of Law in Chicago, Illinois, where he has been teaching since 1981. He teaches evidence, forensic science, and professional responsibility. He has personally tried over 500 jury trials, including 28 capital cases, and currently has a full-time Clinical Criminal practice through the Law Offices of the Chicago-Kent College of Law. Richard Kling is well respected in the Chicago legal community and is regularly  ...[truncated]
Fred F. Herzog | Fred F. Herzog was an Austrian-American jurist and former Dean of IIT Chicago-Kent College of Law and The John Marshall Law School in Chicago.
Ralph Brill | Ralph L. Brill (born December 19, 1935) is Professor of Law at Chicago-Kent College of Law.
